triggerPath: Connected operator clicks Disconnect while streamer.status is streaming or paused.
failureMode: disconnect() tears down polling and closes the serial connection without routing through stopJob() or sending RT_SOFT_RESET.
consequence: The host stops streaming and clears local state while GRBL may continue executing buffered RX/planner commands.
fix: Add a failing running-disconnect test, then make disconnect() reset/refuse while active and preserve idle disconnect behavior.

triggerPath: Run corepack.cmd pnpm lint.
failureMode: Type-aware parser uses tsconfig.json for scripts/assert-correct-repo.mjs, but scripts are not included in the TS project.
consequence: Root lint is red before source rules can be trusted.
fix: Applied: scoped type-aware TS lint to src/**/*.ts(x), added Node globals for scripts/**/*.mjs, and verified corepack.cmd pnpm lint exits 0.

triggerPath: Run corepack.cmd pnpm lint.
failureMode: Function complexity is 14 while the configured maximum is 12.
consequence: Motion-planning estimator logic is harder to review and violates the repo's Karpathy cap.
fix: Applied: split group velocity, fill group block append, cut group block append, and cut-polyline block append helpers; verified planner/estimate tests and lint.

triggerPath: Run corepack.cmd pnpm lint.
failureMode: Function complexity is 14 while the configured maximum is 12.
consequence: Raster G-code emission is harder to audit for laser-off travel and modal power/feed correctness.
fix: Applied: extracted direction dispatch plus forward/reverse row-run helpers while preserving coordinates; verified raster unit/property tests and lint.

triggerPath: Run corepack.cmd pnpm audit.
failureMode: vitest and @vitest/coverage-v8 resolve to vulnerable 3.2.4 versions; patched versions are >=4.1.0.
consequence: Vitest UI or Browser Mode exposure on Windows can become a dev-machine file read/write/execute risk.
fix: Upgrade vitest and @vitest/coverage-v8 together to >=4.1.0 and rerun full verification.

triggerPath: Compare CLAUDE.md test policy with eslint.config.mjs and source inventory.
failureMode: Documented require-test-coverage custom CI rule is not implemented.
consequence: Untested production modules can land while maintainers believe CI blocks them.
fix: Implement the custom gate with explicit exemptions or revise the policy to match actual enforcement.

triggerPath: Compare AUDIT.md status with current gate output.
failureMode: Audit status is stale relative to current test counts, fixed-in-working-tree findings, and the dev dependency audit.
consequence: Release decisions can be made from stale evidence.
fix: Use the dated report as current evidence and refresh AUDIT.md after false-positive rejection and fixes.

triggerPath: Review production TODO comments.
failureMode: TODO references dataUrl purity risk although current implementation uses lumaBase64 and decodeBase64Luma.
consequence: A stale or untracked TODO weakens the pure-core audit trail.
fix: Applied: removed the stale TODO, clarified the separate base64 luma invariant, and verified compile-job tests, typecheck, and lint.

triggerPath: Large image traces through the worker; the worker returns kind:'error' for a request-specific trace exception.
failureMode: Before the current fix, every worker kind:'error' response retired the worker and set workerFailed=true for the whole session.
consequence: One bad trace request could make all later large-image traces throw 'Trace worker is unavailable...' until reload.
fix: Applied: worker.onerror remains fatal, normal kind:'error' responses now reject per request only, and a mocked-worker regression test covers the sequence.

triggerPath: Worker construction, CSP, Vite output, or deployed headers change.
failureMode: Tests cover inline fallback and source-text worker syntax but do not launch the production-built worker on a large image.
consequence: The large-image unavailable-worker regression can pass unit tests and only appear in dev/live browser testing.
fix: Add a browser smoke test against the built app using a synthetic image above the inline pixel cap and assert worker-backed trace succeeds.

triggerPath: Start a job while the connection emits ok during the initial write promise before it resolves.
failureMode: startJob writes the first streamed chunk before storing the stepped streamer state; early acks see streamer=null and are dropped.
consequence: Streamer in-flight accounting can be stale, causing stuck progress, missing follow-up sends, or incorrect RX-buffer accounting.
fix: Add a fast-ack regression test, install provisional streamer state before acknowledgements can arrive, and roll back honestly on initial write failure.

triggerPath: An ack frees buffer space and the subsequent serial write of the next chunk rejects.
failureMode: advanceStream commits stepped.state before the async safeWrite succeeds and then swallows the rejection after logging.
consequence: The app can believe unsent lines are in flight, leaving progress and buffer accounting false after a mid-stream write failure.
fix: Add a follow-up-write-failure regression test and make streamer state advance only after write success or transition to an explicit terminal/error state.

triggerPath: User selects Origin=Center in Device settings and starts or exports a job.
failureMode: runPreflight skips all bounds checks for center-origin devices while the UI exposes center as selectable.
consequence: Center-origin jobs can start/export without proving emitted coordinates fit the physical bed; Frame also uses a conflicting 0..bed bounds model.
fix: Add center-origin preflight/frame tests and implement origin-aware bounds rectangles, including negative center-origin limits.

triggerPath: User changes trace options while a previous preview trace is still running and the older trace resolves last.
failureMode: Freshness token is checked before starting trace work but not before async trace completion calls setState.
consequence: Preview can show stale paths for old options while commit uses current options, breaking preview/output consistency.
fix: Add a stale-promise regression test and check the captured token before setting ready or error state from runTrace.

triggerPath: Open a hand-edited, corrupted, or malicious .lf2 with valid top-level shape but invalid nested device/layer/object fields, including JSON numeric overflow such as linesPerMm: 1e309 or fillOverscanMm: 1e309.
failureMode: Deserializer validates only top-level shape, normalizes a few additive fields, and casts the raw object to Project. normalizeLayer accepts hatchSpacingMm, fillOverscanMm, and linesPerMm with typeof/sign checks but no Number.isFinite or UI-range caps.
consequence: Invalid nested values can survive into compile, preflight, output, UI, or export paths. Numeric overflow can become Infinity, leading to impossible raster buffer allocation or emitted coordinates such as XInfinity that the current bounds regex does not parse as out-of-bounds.
fix: Add invalid-field load tests, including 1e309 layer-number fixtures, then implement schema validation after migration but before returning Project. Clamp imported layer numeric fields to the same finite ranges as the UI and add an invariant test that emitted G-code never contains Infinity or NaN.

triggerPath: Import an SVG geometry element with no stroke attribute, such as a fill-only rect/path.
failureMode: normalizeColor(null) returns black even though the import contract says elements without strokes are skipped.
consequence: Fill-only artwork can become unexpected black line/fill output and differ from the source SVG.
fix: Add fill-only SVG regression tests and either skip absent stroke or implement fill import explicitly.

triggerPath: Import an SVG using group/element transform attributes or style/inherited stroke colors.
failureMode: Parser reads raw geometry and direct stroke attributes only; it does not apply transform stacks or resolve style/inherited stroke.
consequence: Common exported SVGs can import at the wrong position, scale, or color, causing preview/output mismatch and wrong layer assignment.
fix: Add transform/style/inheritance tests, implement transform accumulation, and resolve stroke from direct/style/inherited attributes.

triggerPath: User traces or imports a large vector design, then switches to Preview/Output mode or scrubs the preview.
failureMode: Normal workspace drawing uses a global display polyline budget, but preview rendering walks every sliced toolpath step and strokes each cut step separately.
consequence: The app can be responsive in normal edit mode after a large-trace fix, then freeze again in Output/Preview mode.
fix: Add a preview-mode large-vector regression test and implement a globally budgeted/batched preview renderer.

module: raster image and preview canvas caches
triggerPath: User repeatedly imports, traces, deletes, converts, or changes settings for large bitmap images during one app session.
failureMode: Module-level Map caches keep decoded images, tinted trace-source canvases, and raster-preview canvases with no eviction or scene-liveness pruning.
consequence: Large image data URLs and offscreen canvases can remain strongly referenced after deletion, causing progressive memory growth and slowdown.
fix: Replace unbounded maps with a byte-aware LRU or scene-scoped cache and clear stale entries when scene/project image references change.

triggerPath: Maintainer manually runs the deploy workflow from GitHub Actions on a non-main, stale, or otherwise unverified ref.
failureMode: workflow_dispatch bypasses successful-CI gating and the workflow deploys after only pnpm build:web; it does not run the local repo guard.
consequence: Remote production deploy can publish a manually selected ref without lint, tests, license audit, dependency audit, or repo identity proof.
fix: Run guard:repo in the deploy workflow, require manual deploys to target main, and run/require the same CI gate set before Wrangler deploy.

triggerPath: Electron renderer navigates away from the trusted app origin or opens a new window, then unexpected content requests serial or File System Access permissions.
failureMode: Permission handlers allow by permission string/device type only and no will-navigate or setWindowOpenHandler allowlist keeps content on trusted origins.
consequence: Unexpected content in the default session can reach serial/file picker permission paths, which is unsafe for a laser-controller desktop app.
fix: Scope permission handlers to trusted origins/main frame details and deny unexpected navigation/window creation with regression tests.

triggerPath: Run build:electron-main and package the desktop app.
failureMode: Electron TypeScript emits .js.map files and electron-builder packages dist-electron/**/*.
consequence: Packaged Windows app can expose main-process source structure, comments, paths, and security implementation details.
fix: Disable production Electron source maps or exclude dist-electron/**/*.map from packaged files; upload hidden maps separately if needed.

triggerPath: Audit for old LaserForge 1 code mixing or double-code after the repo mixup.
failureMode: ADR-002 says no code carries over from LF1, but production trace files repeatedly describe the trace pipeline as an LF1 port.
consequence: Maintainers cannot tell whether trace code is clean-room, behavior-parity only, or copied/ported LF1 code, weakening the audit trail for the current regression area.
fix: Run a provenance audit against LF1 trace files; either update ADR-002 honestly for any ported subsystem or rewrite misleading comments to behavior-parity wording.

triggerPath: User imports a bitmap image on an Image-mode output layer and looks at the Start-job estimate, or code estimates a raster-only job.
failureMode: estimateWithPlanner skips raster groups and estimateJobDuration has no separate raster estimator; raster-only jobs total zero seconds.
consequence: Image engraving can appear empty/no-estimate even though it emits real raster G-code and may run for a long time.
fix: Add raster-only and mixed-job duration tests, then implement raster sweep/overscan/pass timing or synthesize raster motion blocks.

triggerPath: User sets a large shape to Fill mode with dense hatching while the Laser panel live estimate is visible.
failureMode: Raw segment budget ignores generated hatches, and the compiled segment budget counts only cut groups, not fill groups.
consequence: Dense fill jobs can still run expensive compile/planner work on the React render path and freeze or slow the UI.
fix: Add dense-fill live-estimate tests, count fill segments in the compiled budget, and add a pre-compile fill-cost guard.

triggerPath: Whole-repo audit for tight-leash small-file discipline.
failureMode: CLAUDE.md says files hard-cap at 400 lines with no exceptions, but ESLint skips blank/comment lines and current production files exceed 400 physical lines.
consequence: Large multi-concern files can keep growing while lint stays green, weakening reviewability.
fix: Make the written rule and lint agree, then split oversized production files around real responsibilities.

triggerPath: Compile an image-mode raster job from a RasterImage with lumaBase64.
failureMode: Pure core calls host global atob, and the pure-core restricted-globals list does not ban atob/btoa.
consequence: In a runtime without atob, raster luma silently decodes as white/S0, so raster output can compile laser-off instead of matching saved data.
fix: Applied: replaced atob with a pure local base64 decoder, added a no-atob regression test, banned atob/btoa from core lint, and verified compile-job tests, typecheck, and lint.

triggerPath: First dynamic import of imagetracerjs or opentype.js rejects.
failureMode: Module-level lazy import promises are cached even when rejected, so subsequent calls reuse the same failure instead of retrying.
consequence: A transient chunk-load/evaluation failure can make Trace or Add Text fail for the rest of the app session until reload.
fix: Clear lazy import caches on rejection, add rejected-first-then-resolved tests, and document/enforce narrow exceptions for module-level caches.

triggerPath: File picker, file read, or save picker throws a non-cancel error.
failureMode: Picker/read failures escape async file actions, and Toolbar/shortcut callers discard the promise with void.
consequence: Operators receive a generic unhandled-rejection toast instead of contextual Could not open/save/import recovery copy.
fix: Catch picker/read phases inside each file action, add mock PlatformAdapter rejection tests, and keep cancel behavior silent.

triggerPath: Open or edit a .lf2 text object whose fontKey is not in the current FONT_REGISTRY, such as a project saved by a future build with a newly bundled font.
failureMode: The renderer loads DEFAULT_FONT_KEY for unknown keys, but the saved TextObject keeps the original unknown fontKey; the picker visually falls back to the first registry entry while no option is selected for the raw value.
consequence: Text geometry can be regenerated with default-font paths while project metadata still claims a different unknown font, and a future build that recognizes that key could interpret the saved object differently.
fix: Add an unknown-font edit regression test, then either normalize the saved key with a substitution toast or preserve the unknown key while refusing silent regeneration until the user selects a bundled font.

triggerPath: Operator clicks Set origin here before LaserForge has a machine-position/WCO sample, later receives an Idle status/settings, then starts or frames from the active custom origin.
failureMode: workOriginActive can be true while wcoCache is null; Start switches to user-origin G-code but skips physical-bed offset validation, and Frame validates un-offset work bounds before sending jogs.
consequence: A job or frame can be accepted relative to a user origin whose physical machine location is unknown, so the app cannot prove the commanded machine-space path stays on the bed.
fix: Add Start/Frame regressions for workOriginActive=true with wcoCache=null, then block with clear copy until an MPos/WCO-derived physical origin is known or the user resets origin.

triggerPath: Operator starts a vendor autofocus command that moves/probes for several seconds, times out, or emits delayed/stale status while the Laser panel remains connected.
failureMode: Autofocus is not represented as a busy operation in LaserState; UI controls disable only on streamer activity/connection state, and the timeout path only returns a warning without stop/reset recovery.
consequence: Home, jog, frame, set-origin, disconnect, or possibly Start can be attempted while a long-running autofocus command is still in flight, relying on firmware rejection or stale status instead of an app-level operation mutex.
fix: Add an autofocusBusy state/lease, disable all other machine-motion actions while it is active, make timeout enter explicit recovery copy, and decide whether timeout should send soft reset or require physical intervention.

triggerPath: USB serial cable is disconnected while a Web Serial connection owns reader/writer locks, then the operator reconnects and tries to select/open the same paired port again.
failureMode: The disconnect event path only marks ctx.closed and notifies close subscribers; closeStreams(), reader.releaseLock(), writer.releaseLock(), port.close(), and port.forget() run only on explicit close().
consequence: The old SerialPort object can retain locked reader/writer resources after cable-yank, making the next close/open sweep best-effort and potentially leaving reconnect to fail with stale open/locked-port errors.
fix: Add a Web Serial mock test for disconnect-event cleanup, release locks in the disconnect/read-loop-finally path without calling forget(), and keep explicit user disconnect as the only permission-revocation path.

file: src/io/gcode/emit-gcode.ts / src/core/preflight/preflight.ts / src/ui/laser/start-job-readiness.ts / src/ui/laser/JobControls.tsx / src/core/job/fill-overscan.ts / src/core/raster/emit-raster.ts
triggerPath: Set Origin is active with known WCO; fill/image overscan emits negative work-coordinate runway while the physical machine-space runway remains inside the bed.
failureMode: emitGcode(...jobOrigin...) emits work-coordinate G-code and runPreflight checks raw X/Y against the machine bed before applying WCO.
consequence: Frame can pass while Start/Burn falsely blocks as out-of-bed, causing operator distrust and incorrect recovery actions; a future fix must still reject true physical overscan outside the bed.
fix: Applied: added user-origin overscan Start regressions, made preflight coordinate-space aware with a WCO/motion offset, blocked user-origin Start/Frame when WCO is unknown, and aligned Frame with Start's custom-origin predicate.

triggerPath: Import or create a closed vector shape, scale it in X/Y, switch its layer to Fill, set hatchSpacingMm, then save/start G-code or preview output.
failureMode: compileJob generates hatch polylines in object-local coordinates before applying the object transform. applyTransform then scales those hatch endpoints, so a layer spacing expressed in millimeters becomes hatchSpacingMm multiplied by the object's transform scale along the hatch-normal direction.
consequence: The layer UI can claim 1.0 mm spacing while scaled objects burn with 2.0 mm or 0.5 mm physical row spacing. That changes burn density, visual fill quality, job time, and material heating without an honest layer setting.
fix: Add scaled-fill compileJob regressions, then generate hatches after applying the object transform in physical scene space or compensate hatchSpacingMm by the transform's effective normal-direction scale. Include non-uniform scale plus hatch-angle coverage and keep preview/output on the same corrected compile path.

triggerPath: Import an SVG containing hidden construction lines, alternate layers, crop guides, or template geometry using display=none, visibility=hidden, opacity=0, stroke-opacity=0, or equivalent inline style while those elements still have a stroke color.
failureMode: walkGeometry iterates every descendant geometry element and calls elementToSubPaths without checking effective visibility or opacity. Color attribution reads only the raw stroke attribute; there is no gate for display, visibility, opacity, stroke-opacity, or inline CSS style equivalents.
consequence: Artwork intentionally invisible in the source SVG can become LaserForge geometry, receive a layer, and be saved/started as real G-code. Hidden guide lines or alternate design layers can be cut or engraved unexpectedly.
fix: Add hidden/transparent SVG import regressions, resolve effective presentation attributes while walking ancestors, and skip geometry whose effective display is none, visibility is hidden/collapse, or effective stroke/overall opacity is zero. Combine this with the KF-017 style/inheritance walker.

module: writeAutosave / startAutosaveLoop / useAutosave / image import serialization
triggerPath: Import one or more large images, continue editing until the project is dirty, then rely on autosave/recovery after a browser crash, forced close, tab discard, or machine restart.
failureMode: Raster objects embed original image bytes as dataUrl in the project model. Autosave serializes the full project into one localStorage value on the interval and before unload. localStorage.setItem quota failures are caught and ignored, and writeAutosave returns void, so the UI has no way to warn that recovery has stopped working.
consequence: The user can believe their image-heavy job is protected by autosave while no recoverable autosave slot exists. A crash or forced close can lose imported images, traced vectors, layer settings, and origin/safety state since the last explicit save.
fix: Add quota-failure regressions, make writeAutosave return a structured success/failure result, surface an operator warning that manual save is required, and move image-heavy autosave storage to IndexedDB or another larger browser/Electron storage lane.
